qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree from a recognized university, duly enrolled as an Advocate with the Bar Council of Calcutta High Court, at least 10 years of experience as an Advocate with substantial experience in handling criminal cases
qualification.details: Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree from a recognized university. Duly enrolled as an Advocate with the Bar Council of Calcutta High Court. At least 10 years of experience as an Advocate with substantial experience in handling criminal cases.

salary_details.pay_scale: Retainer fee Rs. 50,000/- per year, Daily fee Rs. 15,000/- per case per day (Kolkata), Drafting fee Rs. 5,000/- per case, Clerkage 10% of daily fee (max Rs. 3,000/-), Out of pocket expenses Rs. 1,000/-
salary_details.details: Retainer fee: Rs. 50,000/- per year Daily fee: Rs. 15,000/- per case per day (Kolkata) Drafting fee: Rs. 5,000/- per case Clerkage: 10% of daily fee (maximum Rs. 3,000/-) Out of pocket expenses: Rs. 1,000/-

how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the CBI Special Public Prosecutor (Retainer Counsel) Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 30-06-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of CBI to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the CBI Special Public Prosecutor recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your online application for the CBI Special Public Prosecutor (Retainer Counsel) posts is 30th June 2026. Ensure your application is submitted well before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What are the minimum educational qualifications required?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree from a recognized university and be enrolled as an Advocate with the Bar Council of Calcutta High Court.

faq.question: Q3. How much experience is required for this post?
faq.answer: A minimum of 10 years of experience as an Advocate is required, with substantial experience in handling criminal cases.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: There is no application fee for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary structure for the Special Public Prosecutor (Retainer Counsel)?
faq.answer: The salary includes a retainer fee of Rs. 50,000/- per year, a daily fee of Rs. 15,000/- per case per day, a drafting fee of Rs. 5,000/- per case, clerkage, and out-of-pocket expenses.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for this role?
faq.answer: The selection process involves an initial application review followed by document verification.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification does not specify any age limit for the Special Public Prosecutor (Retainer Counsel) positions.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply for this job?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the official CBI website or the direct application link provided in the important links section before the last date.

faq.question: Q9. Where is this position located?
faq.answer: The recruitment is for positions in Kolkata, West Bengal.

faq.question: Q10. What is the nature of this job?
faq.answer: This is a contractual job opportunity with the Central Bureau of Investigation.
